mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST declines yields positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained rise in the federal funds rate signals aggressive policy normalization that suppresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL levels above 220, or the signal's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue projections, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerations consistently coincide with positive 60-day equity returns, or the rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000, it confirms robust labor market strength that sustains consumer spending and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns average negative values in months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200,000, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% across three consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising industrial production and falling real savings indicates a supply-led growth phase funded by consumption drawdowns, which historically precedes equity market corrections and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and RSAFS declines produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of forward return variance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a simultaneous acceleration in housing starts and unemployment decline signals a broad-based economic rebound that lifts cyclical equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive when HOUST rises and UNRATE falls concurrently during the specified regime, or the joint signal's directional accuracy drops below 40% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
